title: Derris glabrescens
taxonomy: Derris glabrescens is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is commonly known as the West African pea tree or West African locust bean. Further classification of Derris glabrescens includes its subfamily, Faboideae. Within this subfamily, Derris glabrescens is part of the tribe Phaseoleae. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally classified as a legume.
uses: Derris glabrescens is traditionally used in various parts of the world for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever and respiratory issues. Its roots and leaves are used in traditional medicine to combat infections, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant is also believed to possess antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it a potential remedy for wound infections and other microbial-related conditions. In addition, Derris glabrescens is used to treat various gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and potential side effects.
benefits: Derris glabrescens, also known as the Brazilian derris, has been found to have various health benefits. The plant is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Its leaves and roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Additionally, Derris glabrescens has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against certain types of bacteria and fungi. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Derris glabrescens.
properties: Derris glabrescens is known for its therapeutic properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In addition, Derris glabrescens has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and as an antiseptic. Its therapeutic properties make it a valuable resource in traditional medicine, particularly in tropical regions.
constituents: Derris glabrescens contains a variety of active constituents, including rotenone and related compounds, which are responsible for its insecticidal properties. These compounds are toxic to insects and have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Rotenone is a natural insecticide that works by disrupting the insect's nervous system, ultimately leading to its death. The plant's active constituents have also been studied for their potential antimalarial and antimicrobial properties. However, further research is needed to fully understand the effects and applications of Derris glabrescens' active constituents.
parts: Derris glabrescens, a plant species, is used in traditional medicine. The medicinal parts of Derris glabrescens include the roots, leaves, and bark. These parts are known to have various medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The roots are often used to treat fever and respiratory issues. The leaves and bark are used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and wounds.
preparations: Derris glabrescens is a plant used in traditional medicine in certain parts of the world. Herbal preparations derived from the plant are believed to have various health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. However, the efficacy and safety of these preparations are not well-documented and may vary depending on the region and preparation method. More research is needed to fully understand the potential uses and effects of Derris glabrescens herbal preparations. In some cultures, the plant is also used in rituals and ceremonies, further highlighting its cultural significance.
side_effects: Derris glabrescens is a plant-based insecticide, and its use can have some side effects due to its toxic properties. The plant contains rotenone, which can cause irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Ingestion of Derris glabrescens or its extracts can be highly toxic and may lead to symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also cause neurological problems and other systemic issues. It is essential to handle Derris glabrescens with caution and follow proper safety guidelines when working with it.
intro: Derris glabrescens is a tropical legume plant native to Central and South America, also known as the "Tambalisa" or "Wild Jesuit bean". The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a natural insecticide and to alleviate symptoms of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Derris glabrescens include rotenone, a toxic alkaloid responsible for its insecticidal properties, as well as other compounds such as isorotenone and desacetylarotenone, which contribute to its medicinal benefits. In traditional herbal medicine, Derris glabrescens is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
